rationale: In the citing paper, Ansari and Mela (2002) appears only in grouped citations as an example of prior work using hierarchical Bayes/MCMC and as general evidence that heterogeneity matters in Web behavior. The authors do not engage the cited paper's customization framework, specific model, or empirical findings in developing their own hypotheses or model structure. Its role is background positioning rather than a distinct, paper-specific contribution to the citing study.
